rulings.Case Title: DANIEL L. BALSAM VS. DSG DIRECT INC., A FLORIDA CORPOATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Ntc Of Mo & Mo For Relief From Order Granting Monetary Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 473(B); Memo Of P&A
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 24, 2011, line 1, DEFENDANT TROPICINKS, LLC, YOUR-INFO INC., DSG DIRECT INCORPORATED'S Amended Ntc Of Mo & Mo For Relief From Order Granting Monetary Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 473(B) IS Granted. Mr. Kelley has demonstrated good cause for the relief he seeks. No opposition filed. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: DANIEL L. BALSAM VS. DSG DIRECT INC., A FLORIDA CORPOATION
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Issue And Monetary Sanctions, And For A Finding Of Contempt Of Court
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, October 24, 2011, line 1, PLAINTIFF DANIEL BALSAM Notice Of Motion For Issue And Monetary Sanctions, And For A Finding Of Contempt Of Court IS Denied. While the title of the motion purports to seek issue sanctions, plaintiff¿s papers do not state what issue sanctions are sought. The portion of the motion seeking monetary sanctions for total non-responsiveness to the prior order¿s requirement that further discovery responses be provided is, in legal effect, a request for a finding of contempt and imposition of contempt sanctions. However, no finding of contempt, much less imposition of any contempt sanctions, can be made without following the specified procedure for contempt, including the issuance of an order to show re contempt, which plaintiff has neither done nor requested. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =302/HEK

rulings.Case Title: BOLLA GODO VS. KAISER PERMANENTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Dr. Alvintang And Dr. Gorton Lee'S Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, October 24, 2011, Line 1. Defendants Dr. Alvin Tang and Dr. Gorton Lee's Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute. Denied without prejudice to being refiled no earlier than December 1, 2011 if either defendant has not been properly served by that date. The policy of the State of California that lawsuits should be decided on their merits, the lack of apparent prejudice to the defendants, and the fact that plaintiff is a self-represented litigant favor giving plaintiff one final opportunity to pursue this action in an expeditious manner. There is no indication in the records reviewed by the court that either Dr. Tang or Dr. Lee has been properly served. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: BONNIE COHEN VS. TINA H. FRANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, October 24, 2011, Line 5. PLAINTIFF BONNIE COHEN MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Cross-defendant Bonnie Cohen¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. Summary judgment is not available because it appears that, even if the cross-claims were adjudicated to be without merit, Cohen¿s own claims against each of the defendants/cross-complainants still remain. Summary adjudication is denied because: 1) Cohen did not comply with the requirements of CRC 3.1350(h) regarding the format of a separate statement in that Cohen¿s separate statement did not delineate any particular issues; 2) Cohen did not satisfy her burden of production that the ¿dog-related¿ and/or ¿furniture in the common areas¿ issues that have arisen since the execution of the settlement agreement are barred by the settlement agreement; and 3) the remaining issues raised by Cohen, even if her substantive positions on them are correct, do not dispose of an entire claim against any cross-complainant and thus are not amenable to summary adjudication. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JERRY B. EPSTEIN VS. ARNOLD SCHWARZENEGGER, GOVERNOR OF THE STATE OF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Monday, October 24, 2011, Line 18. Cross-Defendant California First, LP¿s Demurrer to First Amended Cross-Complaint. Overruled with ten days to answer. Paragraphs 31 and 32 of the cross-complaint sufficiently allege performance of any conditions required to obtain liquidated damages under the parties¿ contract. The determination of whether the February 9 letter satisfies the contractual requirement of a notice of termination is not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. Paragraphs 29 and 36 of the cross-complaint show that the breach of covenant claim is not wholly duplicative of the breach of contract claim. Paragraph 43 of the cross-complaint sufficiently alleges facts that are within the scope of section 9.2 of the parties¿ contract. The prevailing party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)
